claims.text: Black box algorithms optimize for financial outcomes without examining causal mechanism; they identify behaviors that work financially but do not ask how or why, only that they produce results.

Addiction recovery succeeds when the person controls their environmental context to reduce exposure to triggering stimuli; former heroin addicts must avoid the location where they purchased drugs and often must separate from friends who enable the habit.
claims.mechanism: Removing contextual triggers eliminates the conditional cue that activates habitual behavior, making sustained behavioral change possible.
bounded_by: Does not address willpower or motivation alone

examples.text: A former heroin addict is advised to avoid the neighborhood or block where they purchased their drug for life, because exposure to that context reactivates temptation. Similarly, they often must distance themselves from friends who also used, because the social group becomes part of the habit trigger.

practices.text: To change bad habits, modify your context before attempting to change behavior through willpower.
